codeReview.summary: The fix correctly migrates the previously flagged legacy facade methods (postMaterialConsumption/postLaborOverheadApplied/postSimpleJournal) to createStandardJournal and improves supplier-settlement classification in AccountingAuditTrailService, but it does not fully resolve the original scrutiny failures: decomposition is still monolithic via AccountingCoreEngine, production/packing still has a createJournalEntry bypass path, and supplier-settlement action typing in compliance audit remains incomplete.

issues.description: The decomposition failure remains unresolved. Core accounting business logic is still centralized in a single 6212-line engine class (e.g., createJournalEntry at line 450 and settleSupplierInvoices at line 2218), which preserves the prior monolith pattern instead of moving ownership into focused services.

Focused services still primarily delegate to inherited monolith logic (e.g., JournalEntryService returns super.createJournalEntry at line 133; SettlementService delegates to AccountingIdempotencyService at lines 112/117/122/127/132, and AccountingIdempotencyService delegates to super at lines 100/108/112/120/124). This does not satisfy the required real logic migration.

A cross-module packing/production bypass remains: postPackingJournal still builds JournalEntryRequest and calls accountingService.createJournalEntry directly (line 903) instead of createStandardJournal. This path is still used from non-accounting modules (PackingService lines 499/655 and BulkPackingService line 747), so standardization is not complete.

Supplier settlement audit classification remains incomplete in the compliance trail: isSettlementReference only matches SET*/RCPT*/contains(SETTLEMENT) and does not recognize SUP-* references, so supplier settlement journals continue to be recorded as SYSTEM_JOURNAL_CREATED instead of settlement-specific actions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker decomposition procedure still allows monolith relocation (into an Engine/base class) while reporting decomposition success, because it does not explicitly enforce domain-logic ownership in focused services beyond creating wrapper classes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md Step 3 + this fix lineage: AccountingCoreEngine remains 6212 lines while focused services primarily delegate (JournalEntryService line 133, AccountingIdempotencyService lines 100/108/112/120/124).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission decomposition conventions are still bypassable by class renaming in the same layer (e.g., Service -> Engine), leaving enforcement ambiguous for scrutiny and workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md requires real decomposition and ~500-line service sizing, but accounting logic remains concentrated in erp-domain/.../AccountingCoreEngine.java (6212 lines) while AccountingCoreService itself is only 85 lines.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Audit-classification verification guidance appears too narrow; the fix addressed AccountingAuditTrailService but not AccountingComplianceAuditService where settlement action typing is actually emitted for enterprise audit events.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit bc2ce180 changes AccountingAuditTrailService and its tests, but does not modify AccountingComplianceAuditService; AccountingCoreEngine line 807 still calls accountingComplianceAuditService.recordJournalCreation, whose settlement detection at AccountingComplianceAuditService line 306 excludes SUP-* references.

summary: Fail. The fix partially improves journal standardization and audit-trail display classification, but blocking failures remain in real decomposition, complete cross-module standard-journal routing, and supplier-settlement compliance audit action typing.
